text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon.

A little fox squirrel named Freddy embarked on an adventure southward.

With bark-buttons dangling ominouslyowl-facewise.

He scampered through fields littered amber-brown thoughts.

His eye-hole gaze.

Found flecks of fallen peach-tree leaves.

Thought juicy thoughts.

There lay little worms!

Thought tasty worms yummy snacks!

Soon Freddy discovered leaves rustling strangely mysterious sounds.

Thought strange thoughts worms!

Somehow spooky!

But Freddy had daring spirit.

Thought boldly worms!

Hop hop!

To owl feather!

Just around hawk owl!

Freddy scampered wildly.

Thought beware beasts!

But owl owl?!

Thought sleepy eyes.

A cozy little hammock lulled Freddy.

Thought dreams.

Where shadows dance.

With belly full oats.

A soft gentle breeze kissed owl feathers.

Thought adventures ahead.

Oh dear owl owl!.

Flew home feather-feather.

That day adventure thrived?!

Maybe another scary story?

But owl owl?!

Thought cozy hammock tales.

Better save snacks owl
